item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons, and the second image could not be sourced. Thus, the images were removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a lot of great technical information, but the lack of the basics is an issue for me. No mention of the dimensions of the bags, or whether they are uniform in size or not, and the same issue regarding weight. The mention of the Roman numerals begs for explanations of any other differences, size or weight, or at least noting that the bags marked with different numerals have no other differences.

That said, I enjoyed this SCP, a creative addition to the 'jyst add water' market. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ending with "A=43.2[unknown symbol]**2i FUCK YEAH!" underlined twice.

This makes me happy. Having read this at least four times since I joined, I'm still not sure how I feel about it. But the equation coupled with your explanation is enough to tip me towards an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Had to read this a couple times to understand it, but I still like it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes.
Alexylva stuff is essentially misplaced deliveries, so it isn't hard to stumble by. The kids managed to find a bunch of MOLISOLUS sacks. Thinng is, they seem to understand how to use it more than us.

+ show block
– hide block

What happened in the Incident 1043-1 is basically an unterminated loop (killed by fire) - Unlike us, who can only make defined pre-forms, the AWCY guy, I'm thinking an architecture student, has figured out how to program it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get how Alexylva and AWCY both got involved with the same SCP. Did those meddling kids just stumble across a University artifact, or what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They were in the process of working on that when this document was posted, in-universe. Hell, they even know at least one person that must have seen the bugger who was messing with this, but he's probably off to make an exposition at the moment, and fuck if we know where.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would think that once the SCP found the notebook with fingerprints, they would immediately attempt to identify them. If they did, why is there no mention of it? If not, why not? This seems like a fairly obvious course of action they would take.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Arghlblarg. And fix'd. If you can read anything inappropriate on these ones, i'll come and steal your eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Problem: you can see the lettering on the bags on the photo, and it's not at all what the article claims it to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The picture comes from PictureIsUnrelated, you might have seen it there.

No, the comma has sneakt up there as a typo.

As of present, I'm not entirely sure they do.
Back when I thought it up originally it was what determined the default construct, and it probably should have been emphasized more - thing is that means there should have been five structure types but I couldn't think of more than these two. I'll either most likely leave it as it is, or, if good enough ideas come about, write them up and then make it more obvious.
Yeah, this is what happens when you finish off something that has been sitting in your sandbox for three months.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get a strange feeling of deja vu from that image. Was this perhaps posted before and taken down?

numbers "I" to "V" in large red lettering, .

Was something meant to be after the comma?

Final question: do the Roman numerals have any correlation to what structures are built?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I thought this was University stuff! Adding in AWCY threw me off just a bit, but that's really clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I most definitely haven't abandoned it.. it's just since the holidays I dropped from the wagon and couldn't get started up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You finished it! I was afraid you had abandoned this one.

So we're clear for the future, I'm not automatically the final arbiter of all things University. I love this (we'd talked about it in chat…months ago, I don't even remember when) but even if I didn't, the University is an SCP thing. Everybody's free to do whatever they want with it; if it sticks, it sticks, and that's that. Y'all go nuts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I cool yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah. Couldn't find them on the GoI page. Was confused. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really wish I got a hold of that pic before you did. Oh well.

Upvote because this is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the smaller Groups Of Interest. Think artists that use the paranormal to do extreme artisty things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm always interested in University objects, I hope this fares well with Eskobar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All these cross-references.
All this lack of linking to the articles.

But hey, I like it, VAE. It's neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone's gonna have to explain the "Are We Cool Yet?" bit to me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I was getting a University vibe from it. Including "Are We Cool Yet?" was a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sort of, yes.
It's sort of a self-constructed building in powder form.
Looking at this interest group here, Alexylva University and related objects might help you understand what it is about… i'm wondering whether I should make the relationship more explicit. We'll see how the article fares, and when I get an opportunity to chat with Eskobar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So im a little confused. Its a pool of concrete….that turns into hands….that build houses. Is that right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some bits seem, to me, needlessly technical. For example:

If removed from the main pool, samples of SCP-1043-1 cease activity and coalesce, producing an isotropic grey solid of stiffness similar to concrete, but a far higher yield strength - Vickers hardness 50 HD30, but otherwise lacking anomalous properties. Laboratory analysis has revealed the composition to be that of surrounding substrate with SCP-1043 acting as a resin matrix. Initially, an instance of SCP-1043-1 propagates at a rate of approximately ██ m²/hr , assimilating surrounding soil, until reaching a depth of cca. 1.5m and a final area of cca. 8 times the area covered by the resulting SCP-1043-2.

Also, what it does is, ultimately, not very intriguing or creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Arghlblarg!
Thanks for spotting that idiocy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Doesn't" and "shouldn't" seem too colloquial. Should probably be changed to "does not" and "should not".

Also, "…propagates at a rate of approximately ██ m²…" doesn't make sense. Square meters per what, second? Minute? Hour? Day?
